title: Coles/woolworths online shopping
body:
O.P.
Just wondering if many people do their grocery shopping online and have it delivered? I find grocery shopping extremely boring and time consuming, so i'm looking into the online delivery options.
Does anyone know how much it usually costs to get say $100 worth of groceries delivered in a metro area? I had a look on the woolies site, but it just said "anywhere between $5 and 18" which doesn't help. What are these delivery costs based on? The number of items, weight, distance etc?
How good are they for fresh items like fruit and veges etc? Do they generally pick out good ones, or are you better off going into the shop to pick them out yourself?
Also if you live in a unit are they likely to bring them upstairs to your door? Or will they just drop them at the bottom of the stairs?
I'd be very interested to know what people's experiences are. If its much more than $5 for delivery then it might not be worth it.
